title: Array Filter, Map, and Reduce
description: Write a function that takes an array of numbers and returns the sum of squares of only the even numbers. This exercise combines multiple array methods to perform complex data transformations.
instructions: Create a function named `sumOfEvenSquares` that accepts an array of numbers
Filter the array to keep only even numbers
Map each even number to its square
Use reduce to sum all the squared values
Return the final sum (return 0 for empty arrays or arrays with no even numbers)
learningObjectives: Understand filter, map, and reduce operations

hints.content: You can chain array methods together: array.filter().map().reduce(). Start by filtering even numbers (num % 2 === 0).
After filtering even numbers, use map() to square each number (num => num * num), then use reduce() to sum them up.
Complete solution: return numbers.filter(num => num % 2 === 0).map(num => num * num).reduce((sum, num) => sum + num, 0);
